sections.current_situation:
The Commissioners’ and Board Members’ Manual has been revised with a focus on
clarity and organization, ensuring that all relevant requirements for commission service
are clearly articulated, and that the regulations governing the conduct of commission
meetings are precise and easily understood.
Revisions to the Commissioners’ Manual were made in consultation with the City
Attorney’s Office and include recommendations proposed by the Open Government
Commission. Please review the tracked changes version in Attachment 2 for all proposed
modifications. Key updates include:
 Revision of references and content related to creation of Police Accountability
Board (PAB) and sunset of Police Review Commission (PRC)
 Addition of the SAFE STREETS Citizens Oversight Commission created by
Measure FF in 2024
 Inclusion of the new ADA policy for remote participation
 Incorporation of the new teleconferencing policy developed by the City Attorney’s
Office
 Transition to gender-neutral language
 Correction of references related to ad hoc subcommittees following Brown Act
noticing and participation requirements pursuant to Council action
 Updating commission names per the commission reorganization in 2022-2024

2025 Updated Commissioners’ and Board Members’ Manual CONSENT CALENDAR
March 11, 2025
 Revising appendices to align with policy changes (ADA, Stipends)
 Addition of commissions to the list of Alternate Commissioners in accordance with
Council action
 Modified formatting in the Manual to align with Web Content Accessibility
Guidelines (WCAG)
 Changes related to increased use of e-mail and paperless processes
sections.background:
The Commissioners' and Board Members’ Manual serves as a valuable resource for
commissioners, commission secretaries, city staff, and the public. It is provided to new
commissioners, new commission secretaries, and is accessible to the public through the
City Website. As state and local laws evolve and are implemented, updates and
clarifications are necessary. The Manual was last updated in 2019, pursuant to Resolution
No. 69,063-N.S.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects, climate impacts, or sustainability
opportunities associated with the subject of this report.

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
ADOPTING THE UPDATED EDITION OF THE COMMISSIONERS’ MANUAL AND
RESCINDING RESOLUTION No. 69,063-N.S.
WHEREAS, the City of Berkeley Commissioners’ Manual is an important resource for
commissioners, commission secretaries, city staff, and the public; and
WHEREAS, the updated Commissioners’ Manual will provide commissioners and
commission secretaries with relevant and current reference materials regarding policies,
regulations and the conduct of meetings; and
WHEREAS, the Commissioners’ Manual was last updated in 2019.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City of Berkeley Commissioners’ and Board Members’ Manual (Exhibit A) is hereby
adopted and set forth as the official policy of the City of Berkley with regards to the service
of board members and commissioners, board and commission procedures, and the
conduct of meetings.
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that Resolution No. 69,063-N.S. is hereby rescinded.
Exhibit A: Updated 2025 Commissioners’ and Board Members’ Manual
